body: PT Cikarang Listrindo Tbk (POWR) will distribute an interim dividend of USD 28.1 million, or IDR 447 billion, in December 2024, derived from its net profit of USD 72.6 million in September 2024, with an interim dividend yield of 4%. The dividend distribution will occur on December 18, 2024, with a cum-ex dividend date of December 6-9, and the company's stock price closed at IDR 705 per share, marking a 1.44% increase from the opening price.
title: PT Cikarang Listrindo to Distribute USD 28.1 Million Interim Dividend in December 2024

body: PT Amman Mineral Internasional Tbk (AMMN) reported a 958% year-over-year surge in net profit to USD 720 million in Q3 2024, driven by a 117% increase in sales to USD 2.49 billion and a 29% improvement in profit margin. The company's EBITDA also skyrocketed 147% year-over-year to USD 1.47 billion, with copper production up 68% and gold production up 173% compared to the same period in 2023.
title: PT Amman Mineral Internasional's Net Profit Surges 958% Year-on-Year to USD 720 Million in Q3 2024.

body: PT Janu Putera Sejahtera Tbk (AYAM) has seen a 54% increase in its stock price since its IPO on November 30, 2023, with its current stock price standing at IDR 154 per share, resulting in a market capitalization of IDR 616 billion. The company reported a net profit of IDR 6.54 billion in H1 2024, up from IDR 5.33 billion in the same period in 2023, with IPO proceeds allocated towards land acquisition, debt payment, and working capital.
title: PT Janu Putera Sejahtera's stock price surges 54% since IPO, driven by strong financial performance and strategic investments.

body: PT Solusi Kemasan Digital TBK's recent rights issue resulted in share portion changes among investors, with PT Eco Energi Perkasa's stake decreasing from 49% to 48.97%, while PT Sinar Mas Cakrawala acquired 7.5 million shares, marking the biggest share addition. Other notable transactions include PT Daya Kemilau Nusantara Investama selling 50 million shares of PT Teknologi Karya Digital Nusa TBK, and individual investors such as Yulisar Khiat and Sugiman Layanto increasing their stakes in PT Medikaloka Hermina TBK and PT Wintermar Offshore Marine TBK, respectively.
title: Multiple investors in Indonesian companies adjust their shareholdings in various industries.

body: PT Sumber Global Energy Tbk (SGER) secured a potential revenue of USD 10.94 million from Minergy Power Corporation, Philippines, but its stock price has been declining, down 30.90% from IDR 576 per share since the beginning of the year, despite recent contracts worth USD 44.45 million and IDR 3 trillion. As of this morning, SGER's price dropped IDR 4 to IDR 398, nearing an 11 am WIB low, after a 5-day decline from IDR 420 per share.
title: PT Sumber Global Energy's stock price continues to decline despite securing USD 10.94 million revenue from Minergy Power Corporation.

body: PT TBS Energi Utama Tbk (TBS) is set to divest its subsidiaries, PT Gorontalo Listrik Perdana and PT Minahasa Cahaya Lestari, in a transaction projected to bring in fresh funds of USD 144 million, reducing the company's consolidated liabilities by 70%. Foreign investors have responded positively, with a net buy of 26.83 million shares, and despite a recent 8.51% decline in share price, the stock has still risen 47.13% since the beginning of the year to IDR 462 per share.
title: TBS Energi Utama's divestment of subsidiaries sparks positive response from foreign investors, bringing in fresh funds and reducing liabilities.

body: Foreign investors sold a net of 782.81 million shares on Tuesday, November 26, with PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) being the most sold at 1.73 billion shares, resulting in a net volume of -572.88 million shares. Meanwhile, foreign investors bought a significant number of shares in companies such as PT Maharaksa Biru Energi Tbk, PT Buana Lintas Lautan Tbk, PT Darma Henwa Tbk, and PT Lippo Karawaci Tbk, among others.
title: Foreign investors' buy and sell volumes decline in Indonesian market, with net foreign volume reaching -782.81 million shares.

body: Modalku, a fintech P2P lending platform, has recorded a total crowdfunding of IDR 65.15 trillion as of November 2024, with a growth of IDR 1.28 trillion compared to September 2024, and has disbursed loans to productive sectors, mainly wholesale retail trade segments. The platform has approved funding for 5.20 million debtors across five countries, with an average fulfillment rate of 94.5% within five working days, and has disbursed IDR 627.79 billion in Indonesia since the beginning of the year.
title: Modalku's P2P Lending Platform Reaches IDR 65.15 Trillion in Crowdfunding Across Five Countries.

body: PT Bank Sinarmas Tbk (BSIM) saw a significant increase in shares owned by an investor, who now holds nearly 60% of the company, making them the controlling shareholder, after acquiring over 5.91 billion shares. Meanwhile, various other transactions took place, including share purchases and sales by investors and companies, such as PT Sinar Mas Multiartha Tbk, PT Dewi Sri Farmindo Tbk, PT Medikaloka Hermina Tbk, and others, resulting in changes to their ownership stakes.
title: Multiple investors make significant changes to their portfolios in Indonesian companies, including PT Bank Sinarmas Tbk and PT Sinar Mas Multiartha Tbk.

body: PT Waskita Karya Tbk (WSKT) has secured new contract values of IDR 6.8 trillion as of October 2024, still short of its annual target of IDR 14.5 trillion, with management remaining optimistic about achieving the target by December 2024. The company's revenue has slipped 13.2% year-on-year to IDR 6.8 trillion, while its net loss widened 11.7% year-on-year to IDR 3.6 trillion, due to underachievement in new contract values and ongoing restructuring efforts totaling IDR 36.35 trillion.
title: Waskita Karya's new contract values reach IDR 6.8 trillion by October 2024, still short of annual target despite management's optimism.

body: PT Unilever Indonesia Tbk (UNVR) is selling its ice cream business, PT Magnum Ice Cream Indonesia, for IDR 7 trillion, which includes fixed assets, net book value, and inventory value, with the transaction price exceeding the fair value by IDR 460 billion. The sale is part of Unilever Group's plan to separate its ice cream business globally, aiming to realize value for shareholders in the short term, pending shareholder approval at an independent general meeting.
title: Unilever Indonesia to Sell Magnum Ice Cream Business for IDR 7 Trillion as Part of Global Separation Plan.

body: PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk (TAPN) has been granted an extension of its Industrial National Vital Object (OVNI) status for its petrochemical factory in Ciwandan, Banten, until 2029, recognizing its success in reducing Indonesia's dependency on imported petrochemical products. The factory, which spans 115.35 hectares, operates with high safety and sustainability standards, generating olefin, polyolefin, pygas, and other products, and is the only manufacturer of butadiene, MTBE, and butene in Indonesia.
title: Chandra Asri Pacific's Petrochemical Factory in Indonesia Granted National Vital Object Status for Another 5 Years.

body: PT Harta Djaya Karya Tbk (HART) has secured an additional IDR 12.71 billion in working capital from the reallocation of its asset purchase and infrastructure lease budget from IPO proceeds, bringing the total working capital allocation to IDR 45.44 billion. The company's IPO in February 2024 raised IDR 45.44 billion, with IDR 8.72 billion already realized as of June 2024, and the remaining IDR 3.71 billion deposited in a current account at PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA).
title: PT Harta Djaya Karya TBK reallocates entire IPO proceeds of IDR 45.44 billion for working capital.
